overview.description: The Operation Dust Storm campaign targets the transportation sector with a phishing-based attack to disrupt safety mechanisms, leading to potential operational disruptions or physical harm.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: To cause operational disruption and potentially physical harm to transportation systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: Targets mobile users with phishing attacks to solicit sensitive information.
stage.description: The attackers initiate the attack by sending phishing emails to employees of the transportation company. These emails contain malicious links designed to trick recipients into revealing their login credentials. Once compromised, the attackers use these credentials to gain unauthorized access to the organization's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 645 Name: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket) Description: Uses stolen Kerberos tickets to access systems/resources leveraging the Kerberos protocol.
stage.description: After gaining initial access, the attackers escalate their privileges by exploiting a vulnerability in the system's account management mechanism. This allows them to execute malicious code and further penetrate the network, targeting critical systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 390 Name: Bypassing Physical Security Description: Targets physical security measures to disrupt safety protocols.
stage.description: The attackers target the safety mechanisms of the transportation systems, causing operational disruptions or potential physical harm by tampering with critical safety protocols and systems.

Scenario.summary: The Operation Dust Storm campaign exploits phishing techniques to infiltrate transportation systems, escalates privileges, and disrupts safety mechanisms, leading to significant operational impacts.
